questions.question: After 3 steps of constructing the Sierpinski Triangle, how many triangles remain?
questions.answer: 3³ = 27 triangles remain.

questions.question: Write a general expression for the number of triangles after n steps in the Sierpinski Triangle.
questions.answer: The number of triangles is 3ⁿ.

questions.question: After n steps of constructing the Sierpinski Carpet, how many squares remain?
questions.answer: The number of squares is 8ⁿ.

questions.question: If the Koch Snowflake starts with a triangle of 3 sides, how many sides will it have after n steps?
questions.answer: The number of sides is 3 × 4ⁿ.

questions.question: Why does the Koch Snowflake have an infinite perimeter?
questions.answer: Because each iteration adds more segments, increasing the total boundary length indefinitely.

questions.question: Explain why fractals are described as infinitely complex shapes.
questions.answer: Because the recursive process continues indefinitely, creating infinite detail.

questions.question: Find the number of vertices in a prism with a hexagonal base.
questions.answer: Vertices = 2n = 12.

questions.question: Find the number of edges in a pentagonal prism.
questions.answer: Edges = 3n = 15.

questions.question: How many faces does a hexagonal pyramid have?
questions.answer: Faces = n + 1 = 7.

questions.question: Explain why nets help determine the shortest path on the surface of a cube.
questions.answer: Unfolding the cube into a net converts the surface path into a straight line.

questions.question: Why is the shortest path on a cuboid surface not always along its edges?
questions.answer: Because unfolding the cuboid into a net reveals a shorter straight-line path.

questions.question: Explain why projections remove depth information.
questions.answer: Projection maps a 3D object onto a 2D plane, eliminating the depth dimension.

questions.question: Why can different 3D objects produce identical projections?
questions.answer: Because projections ignore depth and only show two dimensions.

questions.question: Explain why shadows approximate projections when the light source is very far away.
questions.answer: Because distant light rays become nearly parallel, producing a projection-like shadow.

questions.question: Why does the isometric projection of a cube appear as a hexagon?
questions.answer: Because three faces are visible simultaneously from the viewing direction.

questions.question: How do isometric grids help maintain proportions in drawings?
questions.answer: They provide equally spaced axes representing length, width and height.

questions.question: Explain why isometric drawings are useful in engineering.
questions.answer: They represent three-dimensional objects clearly while preserving proportions.

questions.question: How can stacked cubes illustrate projection problems?
questions.answer: They show how different arrangements appear from different viewing angles.

questions.question: Explain why visualization skills are important in geometry.
questions.answer: They help understand spatial relationships and solve geometric problems.

questions.question: Why are fractals useful in computer graphics and simulations?
questions.answer: They model natural patterns such as mountains, trees and clouds realistically.

questions.question: Explain recursive construction in fractal geometry.
questions.answer: A rule is applied repeatedly to generate increasingly detailed patterns.

questions.question: Why are fractals useful in modelling natural phenomena?
questions.answer: Many natural structures show repeating irregular patterns similar to fractals.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between nets and surface geometry.
questions.answer: Nets transform 3D surfaces into flat shapes, making geometric analysis easier.

questions.question: Why are multiple projections required to represent a solid accurately?
questions.answer: Because a single projection cannot represent all three dimensions of the object.

questions.question: How do fractals demonstrate the connection between mathematics and art?
questions.answer: They create visually appealing patterns using mathematical rules and symmetry.
